assessment_tools.description_en: A self-administered, symptom-specific screening tool for dysphagia and aspiration risk. Developed by Belafsky et al. (2008).
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一份自我填寫的吞嚥困難及誤吸風險篩查工具。由Belafsky等人(2008)開發。
assessment_tools.reference: Belafsky PC, Mouadeb DA, Rees CJ, Pryor JC, Postma GN, Allen J, Leonard RJ. Validity and Reliability of the Eating Assessment Tool (EAT-10). Ann Otol Rhinol Laryngol. 2008;117(12):919-24.

scoring.interpretation_en: A score of 3 or higher indicates the person may have problems swallowing efficiently and safely. A score of 15 or higher indicates 2.2 times greater likelihood of aspiration.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 總分3分或以上表示患者可能有吞嚥效率及安全方面的問題。總分15分或以上表示誤吸風險增加2.2倍。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem has caused me to lose weight.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題導致我體重減輕。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem interferes with my ability to go out for meals.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題影響了我外出用餐的能力。

items.text_en: Swallowing liquids takes extra effort.
items.text_zh: 吞嚥液體需要額外的努力。

items.text_en: Swallowing solids takes extra effort.
items.text_zh: 吞嚥固體食物需要額外的努力。

items.text_en: Swallowing pills takes extra effort.
items.text_zh: 吞服藥丸需要額外的努力。

items.text_en: Swallowing is painful.
items.text_zh: 吞嚥時感到疼痛。

items.text_en: The pleasure of eating is affected by my swallowing.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題影響了進食的樂趣。

items.text_en: When I swallow food sticks in my throat.
items.text_zh: 吞嚥時食物會卡在喉嚨裡。

items.text_en: I cough when I eat.
items.text_zh: 我進食時會咳嗽。

items.text_en: Swallowing is stressful.
items.text_zh: 吞嚥令我感到有壓力。

assessment_tools.description_en: A 25-item self-administered questionnaire measuring the handicapping effect of dysphagia on emotional, functional, and physical aspects of life. Developed by Silbergleit et al. (2012).
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一份25題的自我填寫問卷，評估吞嚥困難對情緒、功能及身體方面的影響。由Silbergleit等人(2012)開發。
assessment_tools.reference: Silbergleit AK, Schultz L, Jacobson BH, Beardsley T, Johnson AF. The Dysphagia Handicap Index: Development and Validation. Dysphagia. 2012;27:46-52.

severity_question.text_en: On a scale of 1-7, please rate the severity of your swallowing difficulty (1 = normal swallowing, 4 = moderate swallowing problem, 7 = severe swallowing problem).
severity_question.text_zh: 請用1-7分評估你吞嚥困難的嚴重程度（1=正常吞嚥，4=中度吞嚥問題，7=嚴重吞嚥問題）。

scoring.interpretation_en: Higher scores indicate greater perceived handicap from dysphagia. The tool differentiates between dysphagia patients and controls.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 分數越高表示吞嚥困難造成的障礙感越大。此工具可區分吞嚥困難患者與正常人。

items.text_en: I cough when I drink liquids.
items.text_zh: 我喝液體時會咳嗽。

items.text_en: I cough when I eat solid food.
items.text_zh: 我吃固體食物時會咳嗽。

items.text_en: I have to swallow again before food will go down.
items.text_zh: 我需要再吞一次食物才能嚥下去。

items.text_en: Swallowing is difficult for me.
items.text_zh: 吞嚥對我來說很困難。

items.text_en: I drool.
items.text_zh: 我會流口水。

items.text_en: I choke when I take my medication.
items.text_zh: 我服藥時會嗆到。

items.text_en: Food sticks in my throat.
items.text_zh: 食物會卡在我的喉嚨裡。

items.text_en: Food sticks in my mouth.
items.text_zh: 食物會黏在我的口腔裡。

items.text_en: I have difficulty breathing when I eat.
items.text_zh: 我進食時呼吸困難。

items.text_en: I limit my food intake because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我限制了進食量。

items.text_en: My swallowing difficulty has caused me to lose weight.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥困難導致我體重減輕。

items.text_en: I eat less because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我吃得較少。

items.text_en: It takes me longer to eat because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我進食時間變長。

items.text_en: I no longer enjoy eating because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我不再享受進食。

items.text_en: I am not able to eat certain foods because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我無法進食某些食物。

items.text_en: I am not able to eat out at restaurants because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我無法外出到餐廳用餐。

items.text_en: I avoid eating with other people because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我避免與他人一起用餐。

items.text_en: I have difficulty cooking because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我煮食有困難。

items.text_en: I am embarrassed by my eating habits.
items.text_zh: 我對自己的進食習慣感到尷尬。

items.text_en: I feel handicapped because of my swallowing difficulty.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥困難，我感到殘障。

items.text_en: I am upset by my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題令我感到沮喪。

items.text_en: I am annoyed when I have to eat.
items.text_zh: 我在進食時感到煩躁。

items.text_en: My swallowing difficulty depresses me.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥困難令我感到抑鬱。

items.text_en: I worry about getting pneumonia.
items.text_zh: 我擔心患上肺炎。

items.text_en: I fear that I will choke.
items.text_zh: 我害怕會嗆到。

assessment_tools.description_en: A 7-point ordinal scale documenting the functional level of oral intake of food and liquid. Developed by Crary et al. (2005).
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一個7級量表，記錄食物和液體的功能性經口攝取水平。由Crary等人(2005)開發。
assessment_tools.reference: Crary MA, Carnaby Mann GD, Groher ME. Initial psychometric assessment of a functional oral intake scale for dysphagia in stroke patients. Arch Phys Med Rehabil. 2005;86(8):1516-20.

scoring.interpretation_en: Levels 1-3 indicate tube dependence. Levels 4-7 indicate total oral intake with varying degrees of restriction.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 第1-3級表示依賴管飼。第4-7級表示完全經口進食，但限制程度不同。

items.text_en: No oral intake.
items.text_zh: 完全沒有經口攝取。

items.text_en: Tube dependent with minimal/inconsistent oral intake.
items.text_zh: 依賴管飼，經口攝取極少/不穩定。

items.text_en: Tube supplements with consistent oral intake.
items.text_zh: 管飼補充，持續有經口攝取。

items.text_en: Total oral intake of a single consistency.
items.text_zh: 完全經口進食單一質地食物。

items.text_en: Total oral intake of multiple consistencies requiring special preparation.
items.text_zh: 完全經口進食多種質地食物，但需要特別處理。

items.text_en: Total oral intake with no special preparation, but must avoid specific foods or liquid items.
items.text_zh: 完全經口進食，不需特別處理，但必須避免特定食物或液體。

items.text_en: Total oral intake with no restrictions.
items.text_zh: 完全經口進食，沒有任何限制。

assessment_tools.name_zh: 改良版系統可用性量表 (MSUS)
assessment_tools.description_en: An adapted version of the System Usability Scale (Brooke, 1996) modified for evaluating clinical therapy apps. The word 'system' is replaced with 'app' for clinical context.
assessment_tools.description_zh: 改良版的系統可用性量表(Brooke, 1996)，經修改用於評估臨床治療應用程式。將「系統」替換為「應用程式」以適用於臨床情境。
assessment_tools.reference: Brooke J. SUS: A Quick and Dirty Usability Scale. In: Jordan PW, Thomas B, Weerdmeester BA, McClelland IL (eds). Usability Evaluation in Industry. London: Taylor & Francis; 1996:189-194.

scoring.calculation_en: For odd items (1,3,5,7,9): score = response - 1. For even items (2,4,6,8,10): score = 5 - response. Total = sum of all adjusted scores × 2.5. Range: 0-100.
scoring.interpretation_en: Score 0-64: Not acceptable. Score 65-84: Acceptable. Score 85-100: Excellent.

items.text_en: I think that I would like to use this app frequently.
items.text_zh: 我認為我會經常使用這個應用程式。

items.text_en: I found the app unnecessarily complex.
items.text_zh: 我覺得這個應用程式過於複雜。

items.text_en: I thought the app was easy to use.
items.text_zh: 我認為這個應用程式容易使用。

items.text_en: I think that I would need the support of a technical person to be able to use this app.
items.text_zh: 我認為我需要技術人員的支援才能使用這個應用程式。

items.text_en: I found the various functions in this app were well integrated.
items.text_zh: 我認為這個應用程式的各項功能整合得很好。

items.text_en: I thought there was too much inconsistency in this app.
items.text_zh: 我認為這個應用程式有太多不一致的地方。

items.text_en: I would imagine that most people would learn to use this app very quickly.
items.text_zh: 我認為大部分人都能很快學會使用這個應用程式。

items.text_en: I found the app very awkward to use.
items.text_zh: 我覺得使用這個應用程式非常笨拙/不方便。

items.text_en: I felt very confident using the app.
items.text_zh: 我使用這個應用程式時感到非常有信心。

items.text_en: I needed to learn a lot of things before I could get going with this app.
items.text_zh: 在使用這個應用程式之前，我需要學習很多東西。

assessment_tools.description_en: A 44-item patient-centered outcomes tool assessing ten quality-of-life domains related to dysphagia. Developed by McHorney et al. (2002).
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一份44題以患者為中心的結果評估工具，涵蓋十個與吞嚥困難相關的生活質素範疇。由McHorney等人(2002)開發。
assessment_tools.reference: McHorney CA, Robbins J, Lomax K, Rosenbek JC, Chignell K, Kramer AE, Bricker DE. The SWAL-QOL and SWAL-CARE Outcomes Tool for Oropharyngeal Dysphagia in Adults: III. Documentation of Reliability and Validity. Dysphagia. 2002;17:97-114.

scoring.domain_calculation_en: Each domain score = ((sum of item responses - number of items) / (4 × number of items)) × 100. Scores range 0-100, where 0 = extremely impaired and 100 = no impairment.
scoring.domain_calculation_zh: 每個範疇分數 = ((項目回應總和 - 項目數) / (4 × 項目數)) × 100。分數範圍0-100，0=極度受損，100=無損害。
scoring.interpretation_en: Higher scores indicate better swallowing-related quality of life. Scores differentiate normal swallowers from patients with oropharyngeal dysphagia.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 分數越高表示吞嚥相關的生活質素越好。分數可區分正常吞嚥者與口咽吞嚥困難患者。

items.text_en: Dealing with my swallowing problem is very difficult.
items.text_zh: 處理我的吞嚥問題非常困難。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem is a major distraction in my life.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題嚴重影響了我的生活。

items.text_en: I do not know when I am going to be hungry anymore.
items.text_zh: 我不再知道自己何時會感到飢餓。

items.text_en: It is difficult to find foods that I both like and can eat.
items.text_zh: 很難找到我既喜歡又能吃的食物。

items.text_en: It takes me longer to eat than other people.
items.text_zh: 我進食的時間比別人長。

items.text_en: It takes me much longer to eat a meal than it used to.
items.text_zh: 我吃一頓飯的時間比以前長得多。

items.text_en: I take much longer to eat a meal because of my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥問題，我進食一頓飯需要更長時間。

items.text_en: I cough.
items.text_zh: 我會咳嗽。

items.text_en: I choke when I eat food.
items.text_zh: 我進食時會嗆到。

items.text_en: I choke when I drink liquids.
items.text_zh: 我喝液體時會嗆到。

items.text_en: I have thick saliva or phlegm.
items.text_zh: 我有濃稠的唾液或痰。

items.text_en: I gag.
items.text_zh: 我有作嘔反應。

items.text_en: I drool.
items.text_zh: 我會流口水。

items.text_en: I have problems chewing.
items.text_zh: 我咀嚼有困難。

items.text_en: I have excess saliva or phlegm.
items.text_zh: 我有過多的唾液或痰。

items.text_en: I have to clear my throat.
items.text_zh: 我需要清喉嚨。

items.text_en: Food sticks in my throat.
items.text_zh: 食物會卡在喉嚨裡。

items.text_en: Food sticks in my mouth.
items.text_zh: 食物會黏在口腔裡。

items.text_en: Food or liquid dribbles out of my mouth.
items.text_zh: 食物或液體從我的口中漏出。

items.text_en: Food or liquid comes out my nose.
items.text_zh: 食物或液體從我的鼻子出來。

items.text_en: I cough food or liquid out of my mouth when it gets stuck.
items.text_zh: 食物卡住時我會咳出口中的食物或液體。

items.text_en: Figuring out what I can and cannot eat is a problem for me.
items.text_zh: 判斷我能吃和不能吃的食物對我來說是個問題。

items.text_en: It is hard to find foods that I can eat.
items.text_zh: 很難找到我能吃的食物。

items.text_en: People have a hard time understanding me.
items.text_zh: 別人很難聽懂我說話。

items.text_en: It has been difficult for me to speak clearly.
items.text_zh: 我說話不清楚。

items.text_en: I am afraid of choking.
items.text_zh: 我害怕噎到。

items.text_en: I worry about getting pneumonia.
items.text_zh: 我擔心患上肺炎。

items.text_en: I am afraid of eating because I might choke.
items.text_zh: 我因為怕嗆到而害怕進食。

items.text_en: I never know when I am going to choke.
items.text_zh: 我不知道什麼時候會嗆到。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem depresses me.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題令我感到抑鬱。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem frustrates me.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題令我感到沮喪。

items.text_en: I am bothered by my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題困擾着我。

items.text_en: I get impatient dealing with my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 處理吞嚥問題令我不耐煩。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem makes me unhappy.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題令我不開心。

items.text_en: I do not go out to eat because of my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥問題，我不外出進食。

items.text_en: My swallowing problem makes it hard to have a social life.
items.text_zh: 我的吞嚥問題令社交生活變得困難。

items.text_en: My usual work or leisure activities have changed because of my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥問題，我的工作或休閒活動發生了變化。

items.text_en: Social gatherings (like holidays or get-togethers) are not enjoyable because of my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥問題，社交聚會（如假日或聚會）變得不愉快。

items.text_en: My role with family and friends has changed because of my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 因為吞嚥問題，我在家庭和朋友中的角色發生了變化。

items.text_en: I feel weak.
items.text_zh: 我感到虛弱。

items.text_en: I feel tired.
items.text_zh: 我感到疲倦。

items.text_en: I feel exhausted.
items.text_zh: 我感到精疲力竭。

items.text_en: I have difficulty falling asleep.
items.text_zh: 我入睡困難。

items.text_en: I have trouble staying asleep.
items.text_zh: 我難以維持睡眠。

assessment_tools.name_en: SWAL-CARE (Quality of Care and Patient Satisfaction)
assessment_tools.name_zh: SWAL-CARE（照護質素及患者滿意度）
assessment_tools.description_en: A 15-item tool assessing quality of care and patient satisfaction for people with dysphagia. Developed alongside SWAL-QOL by McHorney et al. (2002).
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一份15題的工具，評估吞嚥困難患者的照護質素和滿意度。由McHorney等人(2002)與SWAL-QOL一同開發。
assessment_tools.reference: McHorney CA, Robbins J, Lomax K, Rosenbek JC, Chignell K, Kramer AE, Bricker DE. The SWAL-QOL and SWAL-CARE Outcomes Tool for Oropharyngeal Dysphagia in Adults: III. Documentation of Reliability and Validity. Dysphagia. 2002;17:97-114.

scoring.interpretation_en: Higher scores indicate better perceived quality of care and satisfaction. All scales exhibit excellent internal-consistency reliability.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 分數越高表示患者感受到的照護質素和滿意度越好。所有量表均具有出色的內部一致性信度。

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about your swallowing problem?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的吞嚥問題資訊有多好？

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about the treatment of your swallowing problem?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的吞嚥問題治療資訊有多好？

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about foods that are safe to eat?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的安全食物資訊有多好？

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about how to manage your swallowing problem?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的吞嚥問題管理資訊有多好？

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about liquids that are safe to drink?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的安全液體飲用資訊有多好？

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about what caused your swallowing problem?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的吞嚥問題成因資訊有多好？

items.text_en: How good was the information given to you about how to cope with your swallowing problem?
items.text_zh: 提供給你的應對吞嚥問題資訊有多好？

items.text_en: Were you told about any exercises to help your swallowing?
items.text_zh: 是否有人告訴你有幫助吞嚥的運動？

items.text_en: Were you told about any devices to help your swallowing?
items.text_zh: 是否有人告訴你有幫助吞嚥的設備？

items.text_en: Were you told about any swallowing tricks or maneuvers?
items.text_zh: 是否有人告訴你吞嚥技巧或方法？

items.text_en: I know whom to call if I have a problem with my swallowing.
items.text_zh: 如果吞嚥有問題，我知道可以聯絡誰。

items.text_en: My needs have been fully addressed.
items.text_zh: 我的需求已被充分照顧到。

items.text_en: I know how to deal with my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 我知道如何處理吞嚥問題。

items.text_en: Overall, I am satisfied with the care I have received for my swallowing problem.
items.text_zh: 總體而言，我對收到的吞嚥問題護理感到滿意。

items.text_en: I would recommend this swallowing program to others.
items.text_zh: 我會向他人推薦這個吞嚥治療計劃。

assessment_tools.name_zh: Frenchay構音障礙評估 - 第二版 (FDA-2)
assessment_tools.description_en: A clinician-administered rating scale assessing performance on tasks related to speech function across 8 sections with 28 items. Developed by Enderby & Palmer (2008). NOTE: FDA-2 is a standardized commercial test. The items below represent the SECTIONS and SUB-ITEMS that clinicians rate. Detailed scoring descriptors are in the published manual.
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一個由臨床人員施測的評估量表，評估與語音功能相關的8個部分共28個項目的表現。由Enderby及Palmer(2008)開發。注意：FDA-2是標準化的商業測試。以下項目代表臨床人員評分的各部分及子項目。詳細評分描述請參閱出版手冊。
assessment_tools.reference: Enderby P, Palmer R. FDA-2: Frenchay Dysarthria Assessment - Second Edition. Austin, TX: Pro-Ed; 2008.

e.zh: 極嚴重（無功能/無法評估）

scoring.interpretation_en: Results are recorded on the Rating Form showing strengths and weaknesses. Normative data allow comparison with known dysarthric groups by diagnosis type.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 結果記錄在評分表上，顯示優勢和弱點。常模數據可按診斷類型與已知構音障礙群組進行比較。

items.text_en: Cough
items.text_zh: 咳嗽反射

items.text_en: Swallow
items.text_zh: 吞嚥反射

items.text_en: Dribble/Drool
items.text_zh: 流口水

items.text_en: At rest
items.text_zh: 靜態

items.text_en: In speech
items.text_zh: 說話時

items.text_en: At rest
items.text_zh: 靜態

items.text_en: Spread
items.text_zh: 展開

items.text_en: Seal
items.text_zh: 閉合

items.text_en: Alternate
items.text_zh: 交替動作

items.text_en: In speech
items.text_zh: 說話時

items.text_en: Fluids
items.text_zh: 液體

items.text_en: Maintenance
items.text_zh: 維持

items.text_en: In speech
items.text_zh: 說話時

items.text_en: Time
items.text_zh: 持續時間

items.text_en: Pitch
items.text_zh: 音調

items.text_en: Volume

assessment_tools.description_en: A 20-item patient-centered measure of self-perceived communication effectiveness for people with aphasia and/or dysarthria after stroke. Developed by Long, Hesketh, Paszek, Booth & Bowen (2008). NOTE: COAST is copyright-protected and available free for non-commercial use. Register at click2go.umip.com to access.
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一份20題以患者為中心的量表，評估中風後失語症和/或構音障礙患者自我感知的溝通有效性。由Long, Hesketh, Paszek, Booth & Bowen(2008)開發。注意：COAST受版權保護，可免費用於非商業用途。請在click2go.umip.com註冊以獲取。
assessment_tools.reference: Long AF, Hesketh A, Paszek G, Booth M, Bowen A. Development of a reliable self-report outcome measure for pragmatic trials of communication therapy following stroke: the Communication Outcome after Stroke (COAST) scale. Clin Rehabil. 2008;22:1083-94.

scoring.interpretation_en: Higher scores indicate better self-perceived communication effectiveness. The scale is aphasia-friendly with pictorial support options.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 分數越高表示自我感知的溝通有效性越好。此量表對失語症友善，提供圖片支持選項。

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you show that you mean YES or NO?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地表達「是」或「否」？

items.text_en: Nowadays, how well can you use other ways to help you communicate? (e.g. pointing, writing, gesture)
items.text_zh: 現在，你能多好地使用其他方式幫助你溝通？（例如指示、書寫、手勢）

items.text_en: Nowadays, how easily can you make yourself understood in longer sentences?
items.text_zh: 現在，你能多容易地用較長的句子讓別人理解你？

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you have a chat with someone you know well?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地與熟人聊天？

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you have a short conversation with an unfamiliar person?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地與陌生人進行簡短對話？

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you join in a conversation with a group of people?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地加入一群人的對話？

items.text_en: Nowadays, how well can you show that you don't understand?
items.text_zh: 現在，你能多好地表示你不理解？

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you understand simple spoken information?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地理解簡單的口頭信息？

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you follow a change of subject in conversation?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地跟上對話中話題的轉換？

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you read?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地閱讀？

4.en: As well as before my stroke

items.text_en: In the past week or so how well could you write?
items.text_zh: 在過去一周左右，你能多好地書寫？

4.en: As well as before my stroke

items.text_en: Nowadays, how well can you deal with money?
items.text_zh: 現在，你能多好地處理金錢事務？

items.text_en: How much has your communication changed since just after your stroke?
items.text_zh: 自中風後，你的溝通能力改變了多少？

items.text_en: What do you think about your communication now?
items.text_zh: 你覺得你現在的溝通能力如何？

4.en: As good as before my stroke

items.text_en: How often does confidence about communicating affect what you do?
items.text_zh: 溝通信心多常影響你做的事？

items.text_en: Nowadays, what effect do your speech or language problems have on your family life?
items.text_zh: 現在，你的言語或語言問題對你的家庭生活有什麼影響？

4.en: It's at least as good as before my stroke

items.text_en: Nowadays, what effect do your speech and language problems have on your social life?
items.text_zh: 現在，你的言語和語言問題對你的社交生活有什麼影響？

4.en: It's at least as good as before my stroke

items.text_en: Nowadays, what effect do your speech and language problems have on your interests or hobbies?
items.text_zh: 現在，你的言語和語言問題對你的興趣或愛好有什麼影響？

4.en: It's at least as good as before my stroke

items.text_en: How often do difficulties communicating make you worried or unhappy?
items.text_zh: 溝通困難多常讓你感到擔心或不開心？

items.text_en: How do you rate your overall quality of life?
items.text_zh: 你如何評價你的整體生活質素？

4.en: It's at least as good as before my stroke

assessment_tools.name_zh: 構音障礙治療成效量表 (D-TOMs)
assessment_tools.description_en: A clinician-rated outcome measure based on the ICF framework, rating patients on 4 dimensions using an 11-point ordinal scale (0-5, with half-points). Developed by Enderby & John (1997, 2006, 2015).
assessment_tools.description_zh: 一個基於ICF框架的臨床評估工具，在4個維度上使用11點量表（0-5，含半分）對患者進行評分。由Enderby及John(1997, 2006, 2015)開發。
assessment_tools.reference: Enderby P, John A. Therapy Outcome Measures for Rehabilitation Professionals. 3rd ed. Croydon: J&R Press; 2015.

scoring.interpretation_en: 0 = most severe/profound difficulty. 5 = normal. Half-points provide additional sensitivity. Scores are recorded at admission, during treatment, and at discharge to track change.
scoring.interpretation_zh: 0=最嚴重/極度困難。5=正常。半分提供額外的敏感度。在入院、治療期間和出院時記錄分數以追蹤變化。

dimensions.description_en: Rates the severity of the speech impairment itself (e.g., articulation, phonation, prosody).
dimensions.description_zh: 評估言語損傷本身的嚴重程度（如構音、發聲、韻律）。

levels.label_en: Most severe impairment. No speech output or profoundly unintelligible.
levels.label_zh: 最嚴重損傷。無言語輸出或極度不清晰。

levels.label_en: Severe impairment. Attempts at speech are largely unintelligible.
levels.label_zh: 嚴重損傷。嘗試說話但大部分不清晰。

levels.label_en: Severe/moderate impairment. Speech is significantly impaired but some words are intelligible.
levels.label_zh: 嚴重至中度損傷。言語明顯受損，但部分字詞可以理解。

levels.label_en: Moderate impairment. Speech is impaired but mostly intelligible to familiar listeners.
levels.label_zh: 中度損傷。言語受損，但熟悉的聽者大致能理解。

levels.label_en: Mild impairment. Speech is slightly impaired but intelligible to most listeners.
levels.label_zh: 輕度損傷。言語輕微受損，但大部分聽者能理解。

levels.label_en: Normal. No impairment in speech.
levels.label_zh: 正常。言語無損傷。

dimensions.description_en: Rates the person's ability to communicate their needs and engage in conversational exchanges.
dimensions.description_zh: 評估患者表達需求和參與對話交流的能力。

levels.label_en: Unable to communicate in any way.
levels.label_zh: 完全無法以任何方式溝通。

levels.label_en: Can signal basic needs only with familiar partners.
levels.label_zh: 只能在熟悉的溝通夥伴幫助下表達基本需求。

levels.label_en: Can communicate basic needs and some conversation.
levels.label_zh: 可以溝通基本需求及部分對話。

levels.label_en: Can hold a limited conversation in familiar contexts.
levels.label_zh: 在熟悉的情境中可以進行有限的對話。

levels.label_en: Can communicate effectively in most situations with occasional difficulty.
levels.label_zh: 在大部分情境中能有效溝通，偶有困難。

levels.label_en: Normal communication ability.
levels.label_zh: 正常溝通能力。

dimensions.description_en: Rates the extent to which the person participates in social, vocational, and recreational activities.
dimensions.description_zh: 評估患者參與社交、職業和休閒活動的程度。

levels.label_en: No social, vocational, or recreational participation.
levels.label_zh: 沒有社交、職業或休閒參與。

levels.label_en: Minimal participation limited to close family or carers.
levels.label_zh: 極少參與，僅限於親密家人或照顧者。

levels.label_en: Some participation but significantly restricted.
levels.label_zh: 有一些參與，但明顯受限。

levels.label_en: Participation in familiar activities but avoids unfamiliar situations.
levels.label_zh: 參與熟悉的活動，但避免不熟悉的情境。

levels.label_en: Participates in most activities with some restriction.
levels.label_zh: 參與大部分活動，有些限制。

levels.label_en: Full participation as before.
levels.label_zh: 完全參與，如同以前。

dimensions.description_en: Rates the person's and/or carer's emotional well-being in relation to the speech difficulty.
dimensions.description_zh: 評估患者和/或照顧者因言語困難而產生的情緒健康狀況。

levels.label_en: High and constant distress for patient and/or carer.
levels.label_zh: 患者和/或照顧者持續極度困擾。

levels.label_en: Significant and persistent distress.
levels.label_zh: 明顯且持續的困擾。

levels.label_en: Moderate distress, some coping strategies.
levels.label_zh: 中度困擾，有一些應對策略。

levels.label_en: Mild distress, generally coping.
levels.label_zh: 輕度困擾，大致能應對。

levels.label_en: Occasional concern but generally positive.
levels.label_zh: 偶有擔憂，但整體正面。

levels.label_en: No distress. Normal well-being.
levels.label_zh: 無困擾。正常身心健康。

supabase_table_schema.description: Schema for storing patient assessment responses in Supabase

columns.name: assessment_id
columns.description: e.g. eat_10, dhi, fois, msus, swal_qol, swal_care, fda_2, dip, coast, d_toms

columns.name: scheduled_date
columns.description: When the patient should complete it

columns.name: responses
columns.description: JSON object with item_number: response_value pairs

columns.name: severity_rating
columns.description: For DHI severity question or similar
